title: Debug log PII
description: Troubleshooting adds raw personal data to logs, traces, errors, or prompts.
body: Failure mode: developers add request bodies, user objects, healthcare/payment fields, or support context to operational sinks to diagnose a bug. The data may persist longer than the issue and reach systems with broader access. Detection signals include logger calls with full objects, exception serialization, prompt transcripts, and crash reports containing identifiers. Remediation is synthetic reproduction, field-level redaction, and short-lived local-only diagnostics. Human gate is required for real data exposure. Use when a proposed approach skips personal-data classes, sinks, retention, consent, logs, prompts, and exports or claims success without domain evidence. Do not use this dossier as a positive implementation strategy; it is a do-not-use signal. Actions: block the shortcut, name the missing evidence, and switch to a safer methodology or ask for human approval. Validation signals include absence or contradiction in data inventory, redaction tests, retention checks, export/delete tests, and approval evidence. Risk boundary: treat repeated recurrence as a blocker when it can cause PII disclosure, consent bypass, retention drift, and unapproved processor expansion.
